entries.label: Facility Name:
Address:
City:
State:
Zip:
Date:
Location:
Inspector's Name, Title and Company:
Is housekeeping in all areas in good condition? All areas should be neat, orderly, and clean.
Are all contractors and subcontractors following the environmental rules, permits, and guidelines in place for this project?
Is all project work contained within the limits of work area identified in the permits?
Are the crews only using previously approved access routes for the site?
Are the permits and/or the environmental assessment report available for reference?
Are the “No Smoking” areas free from any evidence of smoking?
Comments:
Are trash receptacles free of the following hazardous, universal, or recyclable waste?
Is waste properly contained in drums?
Are drums in good condition (includes empty drums)?
Is hazardous waste/tanks secondary containment in good condition?
Will the aisles in the hazardous waste satellite and accumulation (storage) areas allow for movement of emergency response equipment?
Is the accumulation start date on all drums in hazardous waste storage/disposal area?
Are used oil drums and tanks properly marked with the words “Used Oil”?
When a hazardous waste drum is “full” has the contractor sent the drum for disposal within 180 days?
Did the contractor receive the shipping and receipt manifests?
Are leaking batteries contained to prevent release of acid?
Comments:
Are all products properly stored and labeled?

Are oxygen cylinders separated from fuel-gas cylinders (acetylene) a minimum distance of 20 feet or by a noncombustible barrier at least 5 feet high having a fire-resistance rating of at least one-half hour?
Are flammable liquids and aerosols stored in flammable cabinets? Are cabinets grounded?
Is the quantity of gasoline storage inside a building < 25 gallons?
Are hazardous materials properly stored away from storm drains or pervious surfaces?
Are storm drains free of hazardous materials?
Are the fire extinguishers, spill cleanup materials and equipment in good condition and in close proximity to where they might be needed?
Comments:
Is the facility area free from any evidence of particulate fallout from stack emissions?
Is the area free from any objectionable odors?
Is excessive noise minimized?
Comments:
Silt Fence Conditions
Are fences free of tears, and appropriately secured to posts?
Is sediment less than 1/3 of fence height?
Is base/bottom edge of silt fence secured below ground?
Do appropriate areas have silt fence?
Are seeding/planting areas and riprap/aggregate areas free of bare spots or washouts?
Are construction entrances/exits mostly free of mud, dirt, and rock?
Comments:
Are the stormwater ponds properly maintained and conveyances free of obstruction?
Do the water discharge areas look clean?
Free of foam?
No oil/grease sheen?
Each outfall clearly labeled?
Is the area free from evidence of industrial wastewater discharging to the ground (e.g., reverse osmosis reject, etc.)?
Comments:
Are the tanks or pipes free from any signs of leakage or spillage?
Are the areas on ground around tanks or piping free from evidence of staining or dying vegetation?
Are oil piping systems properly aligned and are supports/foundations in good condition?
Do all above ground tank structures look sound?
Are storage tank/piping release detection systems free of oil? (e.g., double-bottom tank, monitoring wells, etc.)
Is the cathodic protection working properly? Electrical box shows that amps of current is flowing
Are transformers in proper containment and free of leaks?
Generators holding 55 gallons or more of fuel are required to have secondary containment. If less than 55 gallons of fuel the contractor may use of a secondary containment as a BMP
Comments:
Are Threatened and Endangered species conservation and monitoring plans being implemented effectively?
Surveys of construction area conducted for protected species as required?
Is the site free of any dead or injured animals?
Comments:
Good Practices:
Improvement Opportunities:
Actions Required for "No" Responses:
Inspector's Signature:
